_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize statewide-exec batch: WV AG/SoS/Treasurer/Ag-Comm + WI Lt Gov. State tier, cited, evidence-first.

profile.background: 35th Attorney General of West Virginia (sworn Jan 13, 2025); prior WV State Auditor 2017-2025 and WV House of Delegates 2013-2017.

jb-mccuskey.notes_set: Evidence-state finalization 2026-06-17. Sitting WV AG since Jan 2025 (verified WV AGO/Ballotpedia). Deep HARD record: defends WV near-total abortion ban (GenBioPro v. Raynes); defends Save Women's Sports Act at SCOTUS (WV v. B.P.J.) and beat Biden HHS gender-care rule; led 22-state St. Mary religious-freedom amicus; part of TX-led anti-ESG suit vs BlackRock/Vanguard/State Street; WV arguments adopted in Trump v. CASA; led 20-state WOTUS coalition; moved to dismiss DOJ voter-data demand (state data sovereignty); led McCoy v. ATF brief + 26-state 2A task-force coalition; public anti-Soros-DA/cashless-bail statement; $7.4B Purdue opioid settlement. No cross-party deviation. Null (not false) where no evidence: personhood/IVF/euthanasia, CBDC/balanced-budget, affirmative election mechanics, most border sub-items, personal church. Election-integrity cells null: his voter-data action is a federalism defense, not affirmative voter-ID (scored under refuse_federal_overreach, not election_integrity).

1.note: As AG defends WV's near-total abortion ban / mifepristone restriction in GenBioPro v. Raynes (4th Cir. upheld) — affirmative defense of near-total prohibition.

2.note: Defends Save Women's Sports Act at SCOTUS (WV v. B.P.J.); joined 15-state suit defeating Biden HHS gender-care mandate — affirms biological sex.

4.note: Co-signed DOJ/DOE letters opposing school gender-ideology trainings and secret gender-transition policies.

1.note: Asserts parents' fundamental right re: school gender policies; opposes secret gender-transition policies (Montgomery County brief).

2.note: Co-signed letters opposing K-12 gender-ideology trainings/curricula.

1.note: Led 22-state St. Mary Catholic Parish amicus defending religious preschools' Free Exercise / conscience.

4.note: Part of TX-led 11-state anti-ESG suit vs BlackRock/Vanguard/State Street; secured Vanguard ESG settlement.

0.note: Led 26-state coalition backing law-enforcement / 2A task force during National Police Week — affirmative pro-police advocacy.

1.note: Public statement against 'Soros-backed DAs who are for cashless bail.'

2.note: Helped secure $7.4B Purdue/Sackler opioid settlement (victim restitution); praised HALT Fentanyl Act.

1.note: Defeated Biden HHS gender mandate (15-state suit); led WOTUS coalition restoring state water authority.

2.note: Resisted federal overreach on education/religion (HHS gender mandate, St. Mary religious freedom) and life (B.P.J./Title IX).

3.note: WV's arguments adopted by SCOTUS in Trump v. CASA ending universal injunctions — state sovereignty.

4.note: Moved to dismiss DOJ demand for state voter data; led WOTUS coalition vs federal water/land capture.

2.note: Publicly praised cooperative ICE enforcement, contrasting 'sanctuary chaos' elsewhere.
Led 19-state McCoy v. ATF brief challenging the federal Gun Control Act handgun-purchase age restriction.

4.note: Led 26-state coalition backing a federal 2A Enforcement Task Force; McCoy brief opposes ATF/federal restriction.

profile.background: 31st Secretary of State of West Virginia (sworn Jan 13, 2025; term to 2029). Prior: ED of WV Economic Development Authority, RNC National Committeeman, former WV GOP chairman. Brother of predecessor SoS Mac Warner.

kris-warner.notes_set: Evidence-state finalization 2026-06-17. Sitting WV SoS since Jan 2025 (verified WV SoS/Ballotpedia). Documented record concentrates in his core portfolio: ELECTION INTEGRITY (championed HB 3016 photo voter-ID, SB 86/SB 486 citizen-only voting, voter-roll cleanup of 62,756 ineligible records) and FEDERAL OVERREACH (Feb-Apr 2026 standoff refusing DOJ's demand for unredacted voter data: 'DOJ doesn't have authority to run a state's elections'). Thin denominator (4 scored cells) — null elsewhere is honest evidence-absence, not inference. Cross-party note (not a rubric cell): publicly rejected the '2020 stolen' narrative, breaking with his brother/predecessor Mac Warner. No documented church affiliation.

1.note: Championed HB 3016 (photo voter-ID at polls) and SB 86 / SB 486 (citizen-only voting) enacted 2025.
Declined DOJ demand for unredacted statewide voter data (Feb 2026); rejected HAVA/NVRA as lacking authority.

3.note: 'DOJ doesn't have authority to run a state's elections' — affirmed state sovereignty in the DOJ voter-data suit.

4.note: Refused federal capture of state election data — withheld protected voter info from DOJ.

profile.religion: Bible Center Church (non-denominational), South Charleston WV — member and elder-board member
profile.background: 26th Treasurer of West Virginia (sworn Jan 3, 2025). CPA; prior WV House of Delegates (2020-2022) and WV Secretary of Revenue under Gov. Justice.

larry-pack.notes_set: Evidence-state finalization 2026-06-17. Sitting WV Treasurer since Jan 2025 (verified WV Treasury/Ballotpedia). HARD evidence in his financial portfolio: continued WV anti-ESG bank boycott (added Santander to Restricted Financial Institutions List, Nov 2025) and chairs the Hope Scholarship Board defending WV's universal-eligibility school-choice voucher. SOFT campaign self-labels 'Pro-Life Republican' and 'Pro-2nd Amendment Republican' were NOT scored — too general to map to specific rubric cells (noted for the audit trail). Church elder at Bible Center Church. Null where no documented position (marriage, Christian-liberty action, election, public justice, border, CBDC/crypto).

0.note: Chairs the Hope Scholarship Board (universal-eligibility voucher); pledged to fight any cap/restriction and defend universal eligibility.

2.note: Campaign statement opposing 'liberal efforts to use schools to indoctrinate children with woke curriculum / radical liberal social ideologies.'
Stated 'government should not spend more money than we have'; 2026 agenda of fiscal restraint and returning surplus — opposes deficit spending.

4.note: Continued WV anti-ESG bank boycott; added Santander to Restricted Financial Institutions List citing ESG/fossil-fuel boycott.

profile.background: WV Commissioner of Agriculture since 2017 (re-elected 2020, 2024; term to Jan 2029 — third/final term). Prior WV State Senate 2014-2017; retired USMC Lt. Colonel.

kent-leonhardt.notes_set: Evidence-state finalization 2026-06-17. Sitting WV Ag Commissioner since 2017 (verified Ballotpedia/WV Watch). Scored on named issue-group ratings from his WV Senate tenure (HARD per methodology): West Virginians for Life 100% (2013-2016), WV Citizens Defense League 100% (2016), American Conservative Union 83% (2016); plus cosponsorship of SB 487 (2015 WV Freedom of Conscience Protection Act). 2A cell anchored on the WVCDL 100% rating in the constitutional-carry era (individual SB 347 roll call not independently confirmed — rating is the anchor). Cross-current flag (null, not false): as Commissioner signed a voluntary EPA 'Clean Water, Healthy Farms' cooperative pact and supported USDA ag-data programs — collaborative-with-federal posture, not documented opposition to state sovereignty. Surprisingly no statement found on foreign/Chinese farmland despite portfolio. No documented church.

1.note: West Virginians for Life 100% rating (2013-2016) + WVFL PAC endorsement — documented pro-life voting record.
Cosponsored SB 487 (2015) WV Freedom of Conscience Protection Act (strict scrutiny for burdens on religious exercise).

2.note: American Conservative Union 83% rating (2016); self-described fiscal conservative ('keep budgets in check,' 'no new tax increases').

0.note: WV Citizens Defense League 100% rating (2016) during the constitutional-carry era — pro-2A voting record (rating-anchored; specific SB 347 roll call not independently confirmed).

profile.background: Lieutenant Governor of Wisconsin (sworn Jan 3, 2023; elected 2022 on Gov. Evers's ticket); prior WI State Assembly Dist. 13 (2021-2023). Registered nurse. Declared candidate for GOVERNOR in the 2026 Democratic primary (entered July 25, 2025) after Gov. Evers declined a third term; considered the frontrunner.

sara-rodriguez.notes_set: Evidence-state finalization 2026-06-17. Sitting WI Lt. Governor, now a declared 2026 GOVERNOR candidate (Evers not seeking re-election) — candidacy_status set to running_higher_office; no duplicate governor record found, so no dedup needed. Documented positions are uniformly progressive, all cited: pro-choice (Planned Parenthood Advocates of WI + EMILY's List endorsements; 'enshrine Roe within Wisconsin'); LGBTQ-affirming (sponsored AB 484 gender-neutral marriage/parentage, AJR 72 Transgender Day of Remembrance; HRC/Victory Fund endorsements); opposes school-voucher expansion ('can't afford two school systems'); pro-gun-control (Moms Demand Action Gun Sense Candidate; voted against gun-maker liability shield and against guns on school grounds); pro-sanctuary (2026 LG campaign to ban immigration enforcement at courthouses/hospitals/schools/churches, demand warrants for ICE). Every FALSE is documented contrary evidence; null where no specific position (IVF/euthanasia, no-fault divorce, Christian liberty, CBDC/ESG, election mechanics, cash-bail/police-funding specifics). No cross-party deviation found. No documented church affiliation.

0.note: Pro-choice; frames abortion as a patient-doctor decision and seeks to 'enshrine Roe within Wisconsin' — contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Advocates reproductive freedom / enshrining Roe — opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by EMILY's List and Planned Parenthood Advocates of WI — accepted abortion-industry backing.

0.note: Sponsored AB 484 (2021) adopting gender-neutral marriage/parentage terminology.

1.note: AB 484 gender-neutral marriage; HRC/Victory Fund endorsements — supports same-sex marriage.

2.note: Sponsored AJR 72 (Transgender Day of Remembrance); LGBTQ-affirming — does not affirm immutable biological sex.

4.note: Endorsed by Human Rights Campaign and LGBTQ Victory Fund; sponsored pro-LGBTQ measures.

0.note: Opposes voucher expansion ('we cannot afford to have two separate school systems'); wants to fully fund public schools.

1.note: Campaigned to restrict ICE enforcement and demand judicial warrants — opposes mandatory deportation.

2.note: Called on WI localities to ban immigration enforcement at courthouses/hospitals/schools/churches — pro-sanctuary.

0.note: Pro-gun-control record (Moms Demand Action Gun Sense Candidate) — does not support constitutional carry.

1.note: Pledged 'common sense gun safety reform'; Moms Demand Action endorsed — supports gun restrictions.

2.note: Voted against shielding gun-makers from liability and against guns on school grounds — favors more federal/state gun regulation, not NFA/GCA repeal.
